rationale: Both candidates correctly identify PR #4834 as the causal trigger (matching yenuo26's comment), but both overclaim that the fix is already merged/verified and that the issue can be closed — the actual thread shows this is still unresolved, with Flink-ddd (the #4834 author) asking for a full CI cluster run rather than confirming a fix. Y goes further into unsupported territory, inventing a specific PR title, an exact merge date, and named 'Repro A/B/C' regression tests (test_partial_wake_blocks_generate, test_duplicate_wake_is_idempotent) that appear nowhere in the issue or thread, and explicitly recommends closing the issue — a more confidently wrong stance than X, which at least hedges with 're-run CI, otherwise can be closed.' X's grounding (specific async_omni.py line citations) is comparably solid without the extra fabricated flourishes, making it the more trustworthy of the two.
